question.text: If a beneficial owner seeks to opt-out of a depository, within how many days must the issuer issue new certificates after receiving information from the depository?

answer.text: Within fifteen days of the receipt of information from the depository
Within forty-five days of the receipt of information from the depository
Within thirty days of the receipt of information from the depository
Within sixty days of the receipt of information from the depository

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the Depositories Act, if a beneficial owner seeks to opt-out of a depository, the depository makes appropriate entries and informs the issuer. The issuer, on fulfilment of all conditions as per regulations, shall issue new certificates to the beneficial owner within thirty days of the receipt of information from the depository. **The Trap:** The 30-day period starts from the receipt of information by the issuer from the depository, not from the date the beneficial owner requests opt-out. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision enables rematerialization - converting demat securities back to physical certificates. The issuer must comply within 30 days.
